heroHeadline: WFP: 5.8M Haitians (52%) Face Food Crisis; SCOTUS to Hear Haiti TPS Case April 27; GSF in Early Deployment Phase
heroSubtitle: The World Food Programme's April 2026 Country Brief confirms that 5.8 million Haitians — 52% of the population — face IPC Phase 3 or worse food insecurity through June 2026, with 1.8 million in Phase 4 Emergency conditions. WFP requires $332 million over the next 12 months to sustain operations for 2.7 million people, while the overall 2026 Humanitarian Response Plan remains only ~20% funded ($172M of $880M). The IRC documents that 73% of Haitian households report feeling unsafe where they sleep, and public services have effectively collapsed across gang-controlled areas. On the legal front, the US Supreme Court will hear oral arguments on April 27 in Noem v. Doe / Trump v. Miot — consolidated cases determining the legal status of approximately 350,000 Haitians under Temporary Protected Status; a ruling is expected by late June or early July 2026. The Senate TPS extension battle intensifies as Democratic leader Schumer fast-tracked the House-passed bill (224–204, April 16) while Republican senators pledged to block it, and Rep. Clyde (R-GA) introduced legislation on April 23 to repeal TPS entirely. On the security front, the Gang Suppression Force advance contingent of ~545 personnel (400 Chadian, 70 Salvadoran, 75 Guatemalan) continues joint operations with the Haitian National Police, with full 5,500-member strength expected fall–end 2026. GSF pledges exceeded the authorized ceiling following the April 23 UNSC briefing ($200M+ pledged by 13 states, $59M disbursed, Qatar pledging $30M).
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, Jamaica Gleaner, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre, ICI Haiti, JURIST, International Rescue Committee, NPR, NBC News, Capital FM Kenya, Dominican Today, Security Council Report, UN News, Xinhua, Haiti Sun, SCOTUSblog, Senate Democratic Leadership
